name: Aras Innovator PLM API
what_it_does: Aras Innovator REST and SOAP APIs for open, configurable product lifecycle management (PLM) platform. Enables AI agents to manage product item and BOM data retrieval for engineering and manufacturing, handle change management workflow status tracking for engineering change orders, access document and file vault management for design document retrieval, retrieve project and program management data for NPD (New Product Development), manage manufacturing process plan and routing data, handle quality and non-conformance management data, access supplier portal and collaboration data for supply chain, retrieve product compliance and environmental data for regulatory management, manage subscription notification data for event-driven integration, and integrate PLM data with ERP, MES, and enterprise systems via Aras REST API.
use_cases: Retrieving product BOM from ERP integration agents
Tracking engineering change orders from product development agents
Accessing design documents from product data management agents
Monitoring quality non-conformances from QMS agents
not_for: Simple project management without complex product structure and PLM lifecycle
Non-manufacturing industries without engineering change and BOM management
Cloud-only deployments where Aras on-premise is required
best_when: A manufacturer using Aras Innovator PLM wants AI agents to automate BOM retrieval, change order tracking, document management, quality monitoring, and ERP/MES integration with Aras's flexible data model.
avoid_when: ENGINEERING RISK: Automated Aras item and BOM modification requires engineering authorization — automated change approval bypasses the engineering change process and creates incorrect product builds. Aras's highly configurable data model means standard integration patterns may not apply to your customized Aras instance; always validate against your specific configuration.

auth.notes: Aras Innovator uses OAuth 2.0 and basic auth. Developer resources at aras.com/documentation. ArasLabs open-source community on GitHub. Webhooks (subscriptions) for PLM event notifications. REST and legacy SOAP API. Cloud-hosted (Aras Cloud) and on-premise deployment. Andover, Massachusetts HQ. Open model with subscription-based updates.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community edition available; enterprise features require subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; enterprise support and advanced modules require paid plan
pricing.notes: Andover, Massachusetts. Founded 1996. Private. Open, configurable PLM model. Community edition for SMB and academic. Enterprise edition for manufacturing. Subscription model replaces traditional perpetual license. Strong in aerospace, automotive, and defense. Open community (ArasLabs). Competes with Windchill and ENOVIA for enterprise PLM.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; deployment capacity dependent

known_agent_gotchas: ENGINEERING RISK: Aras item and BOM modifications require engineering change order authorization; automated data writes bypass change control and create unauthorized product changes
Highly configurable data model — Aras data model is customer-configured; standard integration patterns may not apply; always validate against your specific Aras configuration schema
AML vs. REST — Aras has AML (Aras Markup Language) legacy API and newer REST API; REST has better support in newer versions; verify which API version your Aras deployment supports
ITAR data considerations — aerospace and defense Aras instances may contain ITAR technical data; access control and export compliance required
Community vs. enterprise — Aras community edition has limited support and features vs. enterprise; verify edition capabilities before building production integrations
Subscription event model — Aras uses Server Events (subscriptions) for webhooks; subscription configuration is per-item-type; understand event subscription model for event-driven integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: PLM and engineering data. SOC2, ITAR, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Product BOM and engineering change data.

security_score_components.security_notes: PLM. SOC2, ITAR, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Product lifecycle and engineering data.
